binaries.title: mqac.sys (MSMQ AC Kernel Driver) - No security-relevant change; probe direction and size identical across builds (compiler inlining churn)

changed_functions.name: ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive
key_changes: Offset 0x168 (a2[45]): ProbeForRead(ptr, 0x10, 1) in the unpatched build is emitted as an inline MmUserProbeAddress range check of the same size 0x10 in the patched build. Probe direction (Read) and size (0x10) are unchanged; only the inlining differs.
Offsets 0x198 (a2[51]) ProbeForWrite size 4, 0x1A0 (a2[52]) size 1, 0x1A8 (a2[53]) size 1, 0x1B8 (a2[55]) size 8 are identical in both builds.
Read-phase probes (offsets 0x1F0 and above) are ProbeForRead in both builds; in the patched build they are emitted inline. No direction or size change.
All ProbeForRead calls inlined into MmUserProbeAddress range checks in the patched build (toolchain/compiler change, not security-relevant).
changed_functions.security_notes: ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive probes a caller-supplied structure of pointers on the MSMQ message-receive path. Comparing both builds field-by-field, no probe direction and no probe size changed. For the pointer at offset 0x168 the unpatched build calls out-of-line ProbeForRead(ptr, 0x10, 1); the patched build performs the identical validation inline (if (ptr + 0x10 > MmUserProbeAddress || overflow) fault) with the same size 0x10. The inlined shape is identical to the patched inline of offset 0x1F0, which is an out-of-line ProbeForRead in the unpatched build, confirming the 0x168 inline is an inlined ProbeForRead and not a converted ProbeForWrite. The surrounding write-phase probe sizes (0x198=4, 0x1A0=1, 0x1A8=1, 0x1B8=8) are unchanged. There is no ProbeForRead->ProbeForWrite direction change and no probe-size change delivered by the patch.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Unpatched (ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive @ 0x1400234E0), offset 0x168 field:
0x1400237E3  mov  rcx, [rbx+168h]        ; Address
0x1400237EA  mov  r13d, 10h              ; Length = 0x10
0x1400237F0  test rcx, rcx
0x1400237F3  jz   loc_140023807         ; skip if NULL
0x1400237F5  mov  r8d, r14d              ; Alignment = 1
0x1400237F8  mov  edx, r13d              ; Length = 0x10
0x1400237FB  call cs:__imp_ProbeForRead  ; ProbeForRead(ptr, 0x10, 1)

Patched (ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive @ 0x1C0026338), offset 0x168 field (same probe, inlined):
0x1C0026637  mov  rax, [rbx+168h]
0x1C002663E  mov  rdx, cs:MmUserProbeAddress
0x1C0026645  test rax, rax
0x1C0026648  jz   loc_1C002665E         ; skip if NULL
0x1C002664A  mov  rcx, [rdx]            ; rcx = MmUserProbeAddress
0x1C002664D  lea  r8, [rax+10h]         ; end = ptr + 0x10 (size unchanged)
0x1C0026651  cmp  r8, rcx
0x1C0026654  ja   loc_1C002665B
0x1C0026656  cmp  r8, rax
0x1C0026659  jnb  loc_1C002665E         ; no overflow -> ok
0x1C002665B  mov  [rcx], bpl            ; write guard to raise fault on range failure

The patched inline reads from and writes to neither the user pointer [rax]; it only range-checks ptr+0x10 against MmUserProbeAddress. Size 0x10 is unchanged from the unpatched ProbeForRead.

changed_functions.name: ACDeviceControl
key_changes: Main IRP/IOCTL dispatch handler. Register reallocation and control-flow reshuffle.
ProbeForRead calls emitted as inline MmUserProbeAddress checks.
Same IOCTL code comparisons and input-size checks.
changed_functions.security_notes: Main IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL dispatch. Differences are compiler restructuring and probe inlining; same logic and constants. No new or removed security check.

changed_functions.name: sub_140006A58
key_changes: ProbeForRead(arg3, 0x48, 1) emitted as inline MmUserProbeAddress check.
Array index lookup (sub_14000D93C) inlined together with its pre-existing bounds check.
changed_functions.security_notes: The array lookup was already bounds-checked in the unpatched build; the patched build inlines that check. No new security boundary.

changed_functions.name: sub_14000644C
key_changes: ProbeForRead(arg3, 0x18, 1) emitted as inline MmUserProbeAddress check.
Pool allocation wrapper replaced with direct ExAllocatePoolWithTagPriority; free wrapper replaced with ExFreePoolWithTag.
changed_functions.security_notes: Toolchain/pool-wrapper inlining. No security boundary change.

changed_functions.name: ACSendMessage
key_changes: ProbeForRead emitted as inline MmUserProbeAddress check.
changed_functions.security_notes: Compiler hardening and inlining on the message-send path. This function does not reach ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive. No new security logic.

security_findings.entry_point: IOCTL dispatch via the MSMQ AC device (\\.\MQAC), message-receive path (ACDeviceControl -> CPacket::GetProperties -> ACDeepProbeReceiveParams -> ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive). Requires the Message Queuing feature installed.
security_findings.description: The original report claimed the patch converts a ProbeForRead into a ProbeForWrite at offset 0x168 and corrects several probe sizes. Verification against both builds shows this is not the case. At offset 0x168 the probe is ProbeForRead(ptr, 0x10, 1) in the unpatched build and an inlined MmUserProbeAddress range check of the same size 0x10 in the patched build; direction (Read) and size (0x10) are unchanged. The surrounding write-phase probe sizes (0x198=4, 0x1A0=1, 0x1A8=1, 0x1B8=8) are identical in both builds, and there is no probe at offset 0x1B0. The single difference is that one ProbeForRead call is emitted inline in the patched build, matching the toolchain-wide pattern of inlining ProbeForRead/ProbeForWrite into MmUserProbeAddress checks. No probe direction or size was changed by the patch.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable. No behavioral difference exists between the builds in this function.
security_findings.primitive: None. No exploit primitive is delivered or removed by the patch. There is no demonstrated kernel write to a read-only page, no out-of-bounds write, and no denial-of-service primitive attributable to the change.
security_findings.evidence:
Unpatched ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive (decompiled):
  v29 = a2[45];                          // offset 0x168
  if (v29) ProbeForRead(v29, 0x10u, 1u);
  ...
  v33 = a2[52]; if (v33) ProbeForWrite(v33, 1u, 1u);   // offset 0x1A0
  v34 = a2[53]; if (v34) ProbeForWrite(v34, 1u, 1u);   // offset 0x1A8
  v35 = a2[55]; if (v35) ProbeForWrite(v35, 8u, 1u);   // offset 0x1B8

Patched ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive (decompiled), offset 0x168:
  v30 = (unsigned __int64 *)MmUserProbeAddress;
  if (v29 != 0 && (v29 + 16 > MmUserProbeAddress || v29 + 16 < v29))
      *(_BYTE *)MmUserProbeAddress = 0;   // inlined ProbeForRead, size 0x10 unchanged
  ... offsets 0x1A0/0x1A8/0x1B8 remain ProbeForWrite(1)/ProbeForWrite(1)/ProbeForWrite(8), unchanged.

The patched inline at offset 0x168 is identical in shape to the patched inline of offset 0x1F0, which is an out-of-line ProbeForRead(ptr, 4, 1) in the unpatched build, confirming the 0x168 inline is an inlined ProbeForRead and not a ProbeForWrite.
security_findings.assembly:
Unpatched, ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive @ 0x1400234E0, offset 0x168:
0x1400237E3  mov  rcx, [rbx+168h]        ; Address
0x1400237EA  mov  r13d, 10h              ; Length = 0x10
0x1400237F0  test rcx, rcx
0x1400237F3  jz   loc_140023807
0x1400237F5  mov  r8d, r14d              ; Alignment = 1
0x1400237F8  mov  edx, r13d              ; Length = 0x10
0x1400237FB  call cs:__imp_ProbeForRead  ; ProbeForRead(ptr, 0x10, 1)

Patched, ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive @ 0x1C0026338, offset 0x168 (same probe, inlined):
0x1C0026637  mov  rax, [rbx+168h]
0x1C002663E  mov  rdx, cs:MmUserProbeAddress
0x1C0026645  test rax, rax
0x1C0026648  jz   loc_1C002665E
0x1C002664A  mov  rcx, [rdx]
0x1C002664D  lea  r8, [rax+10h]         ; size 0x10 unchanged
0x1C0026651  cmp  r8, rcx
0x1C0026654  ja   loc_1C002665B
0x1C0026656  cmp  r8, rax
0x1C0026659  jnb  loc_1C002665E
0x1C002665B  mov  [rcx], bpl

summary: No security-relevant change is present between the two builds. The original report claimed the patch fixes a ProbeForRead->ProbeForWrite direction mismatch at struct offset 0x168 and corrects several undersized/oversized probes in ACDeepProbeMsgPropsForReceive. Verification against both builds disproves this: at offset 0x168 the probe is ProbeForRead(ptr, 0x10, 1) in the unpatched build and an inlined MmUserProbeAddress range check of the same size 0x10 in the patched build (an inlined ProbeForRead, confirmed by the identical inline shape of offset 0x1F0 which is ProbeForRead in the unpatched build). The surrounding write-phase probe sizes are identical in both builds and there is no probe at offset 0x1B0. The two files are different toolchain/servicing builds whose 418 changed functions differ predominantly by ProbeForRead/ProbeForWrite inlining into MmUserProbeAddress checks, register reallocation, and pool/memset wrapper inlining. No probe direction or size changed and no exploit primitive is introduced or removed.
